texts.orig: A B S T R A C T
texts.text: A B S T R A C T

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Extracting lithium from brines is challenging due to the high Mg 2 + /Li + ratio and low Li + concentration. In this paper,  a  selective  electrodialysis  process  (S-ED)  with  feed  and  bleed  mode  was  developed  to  extract  Li + continuously from brines. Take the system performance including the recovery ratio of Li + ( R Li), leakage ratio of Mg 2 + ( L Mg), migration rate ( M r), specific energy consumption ( E SEC), current efficiency ( η ) and separation coefficient of Li + and Mg 2 + ( F Li-Mg) as evaluation parameters, the typical factors such as applied voltage, supplementary feed flow rate, and linear flow velocity were investigated. In addition, the Li + extraction parameters of different feed solution species in the S-ED process were compared under optimization operating conditions and Li2CO3  was obtained from Li-rich solution by precipitation reaction. The results showed that the increase of voltage could promote R Li, but the excessive voltage caused a decrease in F Li-Mg. The increase of the linear flow velocity  could increase M r  but hardly affected F Li-Mg  and E SEC.  The  increase  in  the  supplementary  feed  flow increased F Li-Mg. For different feeding solutions, it was found that extracting Li + from bittern had good economic value and high purity Li2CO3 was obtained after S-ED. Compared with the traditional process, the S-ED process with the ' feed and bleed ' mode proposed in this article has the advantages of simple operation, continuity, and environmental friendliness. It is a promising technology that can continuously enrich Li + from brines.

The key challenge of extracting lithium from salt lake brine is restricted by the low concentration of Li + and high Mg 2 + /Li + ratio of salt lake resources. At present, methods of lithium extraction include precipitation [6,7], solvent extraction [8,9], ion exchange adsorption [10,11], and membrane processes (including nanofiltration  and  electrodialysis)  [12 -16],  etc.  As  a  traditional  lithium extraction method, the precipitation method has a great application in the precipitation of Li2CO3 from brine with a low Mg 2 + /Li + ratio while it requires a large amount of precipitation agent. texts.orig: nanofiltration on Mg 2 + is not high for the brine with high ion concentration, and the separation effect is greatly affected by steric hindrance [18]. Both the adsorption methods and solvent extraction methods have good Li + selectivity. However, these two methods still have a series of technical problems to be solved. The adsorption methods need to further enhance the permeability of the powdered adsorbent, reduce dissolution loss, and increase the effectiveness of the sorbent granulation [19 -21]. The current solvent extraction method needs to be improved [22]. The electrodialysis system equipped with monovalent selective ion exchange membranes, namely selective-electrodialysis (S-ED), can separate and concentrate lithium from high Mg 2 + /Li + ratio brines [23,24]. The S-ED process has no phase change process and low energy consumption, thus greatly simplifies the process flow and has good industrial application prospects.

The electrodialysis system equipped with monovalent selective ion exchange membranes, namely selective-electrodialysis (S-ED), can separate and concentrate lithium from high Mg 2 + /Li + ratio brines [23,24]. The S-ED process has no phase change process and low energy consumption, thus greatly simplifies the process flow and has good industrial application prospects.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In recent years, a large number of studies on ion-exchange membranes of electrodialysis have been focused on the separation of Mg 2 + / Li + [25]. For example, Nie et al. [26] used electrodialysis to treat brines with a high Mg 2 + /Li + ratio, and the recovery ratio of Li + was over 90%. Dall and Boateng [27] concentrated lithium from the high Mg 2 + /Li + ratio brine with the Mg 2 + /Li + ratio being 60, in which the Mg 2 + /Li + ratio in the brine was reduced to 5 and the recovery ratio of Li + was up to 78.9%  after  multistage  electrodialysis.  Furthermore,  Zhou  et  al.[28] reported  that  using  a  two-stage  electrodialysis,  the  final  Li2SO4  concentration could reach as high as 17.4%. These studies indicate that the electrodialysis technique can achieve a high separation coefficient from Mg 2 + to Li + . Previously, the effect of some operating parameters like the voltage, linear flow velocity, pH, and Mg 2 + /Li + ratio for the separation of Li + and Mg 2 + in binary cations solution system based on batch S-ED has  been  explored.  Under  the  optimized  experimental  conditions, lithium resource was recovered from brine with the high Mg 2 + /Li + ratio of  60,  and  the  Mg 2 + /Li + ratio  was  reduced to 7 [29], which can be classified as low Mg 2 + /Li + ratio category [30].

These studies indicate that the electrodialysis technique can achieve a high separation coefficient from Mg 2 + to Li + . Under  the  optimized  experimental  conditions, lithium resource was recovered from brine with the high Mg 2 + /Li + ratio of  60,  and  the  Mg 2 + /Li + ratio  was  reduced to 7 [29], which can be classified as low Mg 2 + /Li + ratio category [30].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All  the  above  show  that  S-ED  method  can  effectively  reduce  the Mg 2 + /Li + ratio  of  the  salt  lake  brine.  But  existing  studies  are  still incomplete. Most of the studies focused on the study of the characteristics and mechanisms of batch S-ED [31] for Mg-Li separation, making the practical application of Li + extraction processes based on S-ED Mg-Li pre-separation  technology  still  lack  continuous  and  systematic  data support. Therefore, it is essential to investigate the S-ED process with ' bleed mode ' to achieve the continuous enrichment of Li + from the high Mg 2 + /Li + ratio brine.

Most of the studies focused on the study of the characteristics and mechanisms of batch S-ED [31] for Mg-Li separation, making the practical application of Li + extraction processes based on S-ED Mg-Li pre-separation  technology  still  lack  continuous  and  systematic  data support. Therefore, it is essential to investigate the S-ED process with ' bleed mode ' to achieve the continuous enrichment of Li + from the high Mg 2 + /Li + ratio brine.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this paper, based on the feed and bleed mode [32], an external water supplement of diluting tank and two overflow ports of both the concentrating tank and diluting tank are added to the internal circulation of the membrane stack to achieve continuous enrichment of Li + with S-ED. The comparison of Li + extraction performance between batch S-ED and continuous S-ED with feed and bleed mode was investigated first. Then, the effect of applied voltage, linear flow velocity, and supplementary feed flow rate of the continuous S-ED operating process was discussed.  Furthermore,  the  treatment  effect  of  feed  solution  was analyzed  and  Li2CO3 was  obtained  by  precipitation  reaction.  This research provides data support for the extraction of lithium resources in brines  and  the  continuous  industrial  production  of  Li2CO3  based  on S-ED.

texts.text: 2.2. Experimental apparatus

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The electrodialysis apparatus mainly consists of a membrane stack, diluting tank, concentrating tank, electrolyte tank, centrifugal pump, peristaltic pump, direct-current (DC) power, as shown in Fig. 1. The core component of the continuous S-ED process is the membrane stack, which contains 10 pairs of monovalent selective ion-exchange membranes with an effective surface area of 0.01407 m 2 , whose performance parameters were shown in reference [24]. The electricity is provided by a regulated DC power supply and the experiment is carried out in a constant voltage mode. There is a new overflow port on the diluting tank and concentrating tank. The feed solution was pumped into a diluting tank with a peristaltic pump.

texts.orig: 2.3. Experimental process
texts.text: 2.3. Experimental process

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The experimental process based on the continuous S-ED process of preparing Li2CO3 from brine is shown in Fig. 2. For the continuous S-ED process, in the initial stage, the diluting tank and concentrating tank were respectively filled with 3 L feed solution and 3 L 0.5 mol/L NaCl solution.  The  electrolyte  tank  was  filled  with  a  3 L  5%  (mass  ratio) Na2SO4  solution, and the cathode compartment shared the same electrolyte tank with the anode compartment. During the experiment, feed solution,  NaCl  solution,  and  Na2SO4 solution  were  independently conveyed by three pumps, and the flux was measured by the rotameter. The experiment was carried out by continuously supplying feed solution to the diluting tank to maintain the ion content. As the hydrated ions migrate and supplement, both the Li-rich solution and diluting solution flow out from the corresponding overflow port.
texts.text: The experimental process based on the continuous S-ED process of preparing Li2CO3 from brine is shown in Fig. 2. For the continuous S-ED process, in the initial stage, the diluting tank and concentrating tank were respectively filled with 3 L feed solution and 3 L 0.5 mol/L NaCl solution.  The  electrolyte  tank  was  filled  with  a  3 L  5%  (mass  ratio) Na2SO4  solution, and the cathode compartment shared the same electrolyte tank with the anode compartment. During the experiment, feed solution,  NaCl  solution,  and  Na2SO4 solution  were  independently conveyed by three pumps, and the flux was measured by the rotameter. The experiment was carried out by continuously supplying feed solution to the diluting tank to maintain the ion content. As the hydrated ions migrate and supplement, both the Li-rich solution and diluting solution flow out from the corresponding overflow port.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The experiments were carried out at room temperature. Firstly, the effect  of  batch  S-ED  and  continuous  S-ED  on  Li + extraction  were compared under the same operating conditions. Then, using the salt lake brine as feed solution, the effects of applied voltage (8 -12 V), the supplementary flow rate (1 -4 L/h), and linear flow velocity (2.38 -5.56 cm/ s) were investigated using a continuous S-ED process with feed and bleed mode. Finally, using the optimal conditions obtained in the foregoing, SED experiments were performed with artificial brine and bittern diluted 2, 3, and 4 times as feed solutions to study the effects of feed solution species.

texts.orig: 3.1. Comparison of Li + extraction performance between batch S-ED and S-ED with feed and bleed mode
texts.text: 3.1. Comparison of Li + extraction performance between batch S-ED and S-ED with feed and bleed mode

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Under the working condition of "voltage 10 V, linear flow velocity 4.76 cm/s", the batch S-ED and S-ED with feed and bleed mode experiment was carried out with salt lake brine as feed solution. The supplementary feed flow rate in the continuous operation process is 2 L/h.
texts.text: Under the working condition of "voltage 10 V, linear flow velocity 4.76 cm/s", the batch S-ED and S-ED with feed and bleed mode experiment was carried out with salt lake brine as feed solution. The supplementary feed flow rate in the continuous operation process is 2 L/h.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The parameters of Li + extraction were calculated after 180 min of batch S-ED. After 180 min of operation, the concentration of Li + in the concentrating tank almost did not increase. Under long-term operating conditions,  the  concentration  of  the  concentrating  tank  continues  to increase and eventually reaches a limit, at which time the system reaches a dynamic equilibrium state. The data of the S-ED process was the average value of 5 h after the experiment reaches a steady state. The data of continuous operation was the average value of each parameter 5 h after the steady state was reached. A comparison of parameters is shown in Table 2.

The data of continuous operation was the average value of each parameter 5 h after the steady state was reached. A comparison of parameters is shown in Table 2.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It can be seen from Table 2 that it showed a bigger value of R Li and a higher value of L Mg in the batch S-ED process than that in the feed and bleed  mode.  Apparently,  under  batch  S-ED  operating  process,  the limited Li + migrated relatively fully to the concentrating tank in the absence of exogenous supplement. However, with the increase of time, the proportion of Mg 2 + in the diluting tank increased, and the competitive migration of Mg 2 + increased accordingly, leading to a high L Mg, which further affected η of Li + extraction and the final F Li-Mg. In the process  of  continuous  operation,  the  Li + were  continuously  supplemented, so that Li + could migrate with high flux, thus ensuring the high η and high F Li-Mg. The Li + -Mg 2 + migration diagram of batch S-ED and SED with feed and bleed mode was shown in Fig. 3. It is also found that E SEC  of continuous operation is not significantly different from that of batch operation, so the continuous operation has better industrialization potential.

texts.orig: The effect of operating voltage is shown in Fig. 4. It can be seen from Fig. 4a that both M r(Li)  and M r(Mg)  increased with the increase of the operating voltage. Fig. 4b visually shows the effect of operating voltage on R Li  and L Mg.  When  the  voltage  was  increased, R Li  and L Mg  had increased to different degrees, which was consistent with the trend of migration rate. It can be seen from Fig. 4c that when the voltage was 10 V, η was the highest. This might be because the competitive migration of coexisting ions was relatively weak at a voltage of 10 V. Under the operating voltage of 12 V, E SEC value was significantly increased. It can be concluded that the increase in voltage is beneficial to promote ion migration and thus increase the recovery ratio of lithium ions. However, the excessive voltage also promotes the leaky migration of other coexisting ions, thereby reducing the current efficiency of Li + , which is not conducive to the purification of Li + .  As  shown in  Fig.  4a,  When  the operating voltage was increased from 8 V to 10 V, the migration rates of Li + and  Mg 2 + increased  from  28.1 mmol/(m 2 ⋅ min)  and  62.9 mmol/ (m 2 ⋅ min)  to  44.9 mmol/(m 2 ⋅ min)  and  73.0 mmol/(m 2 ⋅ min),  respectively. However, when the operating voltage was further increased to 12 V, M r(Mg) was  significantly  increased  to  147.6 mmol/(m 2 ⋅ min) whereas M r(Li)  remained relatively less change (46.3 mmol/(m 2 ⋅ min)), which was because the content of Mg 2 + was higher, and under a larger driving force, its competitive migration was significantly enhanced. It resulted in a decrease of the F Li-Mg, which can be seen from Fig. 4d. Therefore, the operating voltage selected in this paper is 10 V.

texts.orig: 3.2.2. Effect of linear flow velocity
texts.text: 3.2.2. Effect of linear flow velocity

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Linear flow velocity plays an important role in mass transfer in the basic unit of electrodialysis. It will affect the residence time of ions and the thickness of the concentration boundary layer, thus affecting the directional migration of ions [25]. The effect of the linear flow velocity of  2.38 -5.56 cm/s  was  studied  at  the  voltage  of  10 V  and  a  supplementary feed flow of 2 L/h.
texts.text: Linear flow velocity plays an important role in mass transfer in the basic unit of electrodialysis. It will affect the residence time of ions and the thickness of the concentration boundary layer, thus affecting the directional migration of ions [25]. The effect of the linear flow velocity of  2.38 -5.56 cm/s  was  studied  at  the  voltage  of  10 V  and  a  supplementary feed flow of 2 L/h.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The effect of linear flow velocity is shown in Fig. 5. It can be seen from Fig. 5a that M r(Li)  and M r(Mg)  increased first and then decreased with the increase of linear flow velocity. When the linear flow velocity was  4.76 cm/s, M r(Li) and M r(Mg) reached  the  highest  values  of 44.9 mmol/(m 2 ⋅ min) and 73.0 mmol/(m 2 ⋅ min), respectively. With the increase of linear flow velocity, the turbulence degree of fluid flow increases, the boundary layer between membrane and solution became thinner, which accelerated the ion migration rate. [38]. Similarly, as can be seen from Fig. 5b, R Li and L Mg increased first and then decreased with the increase of linear flow velocity. When the linear flow velocity was 4.76 cm/s, R Li  and L Mg  reached the highest value of 68.0% and 5.9% respectively.  Since  the  change  of  linear  flow  velocity  will  affect  the migration of Li + and Mg 2 + at the same time, and the influence trend is the same, it can be seen from Fig. 5d that the change of linear flow velocity has little effect on F Li-Mg.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Supplementary  feed  flow  rate  is  an  indispensable  factor  to  be considered in the S-ED process. In this part of the experiment, the influence of supplementary feed flow (1 -4 L/h) was investigated at the voltage of 10 V and linear flow velocity of 4.76 cm/s.
texts.text: Supplementary  feed  flow  rate  is  an  indispensable  factor  to  be considered in the S-ED process. In this part of the experiment, the influence of supplementary feed flow (1 -4 L/h) was investigated at the voltage of 10 V and linear flow velocity of 4.76 cm/s.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It can be seen from Fig. 6a that M r(Li) increased with the increase of the supplementary feed flow, and M r(Mg) showed the maximum when the supplementary feed flow was 1 L/h, and the change became smaller as the supplementary feed flow increased further. Because the increase of the supplementary feed flow rate increased the ionic concentration in the diluting tank, the solution resistance is significantly reduced and the operating  current  is  increased,  thereby  objectively  increasing  the driving force for ion migration in the diluting tank. With the increase of the supplementary feed flow rate, the ions in the diluting tank were supplemented quickly, which promoted the acceleration of ion migration.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As shown in Fig. 6b, R Li  decreased with the increase of the supplementary feed flow. When the supplementary flow rate was 1 L/h, R Li could be larger than 80%. The large supplementary feed flow led to the short residence time of the solution in the diluting tank and the concentration of each ion in the diluting tank was increased in different degrees. Hence, Li + in the solution would not migrate to the concentrating tank but overflow from the overflow outlet, which resulted in a decrease in R Li. The increase of supplementary feed flow increased the Li + content in the solution, which led to the increase of F Li-Mg  (Fig. 6d).

texts.orig: The solution chemical characteristics of brine also have a great influence  on  the  Li + extraction  process  of  S-ED,  so  the  Li + extraction performance of salt lake brine and bittern was investigated. In addition, the excessive concentration of bittern may cause corrosion and loss of the electrodialysis equipment and membrane stack, so the bittern should be diluted. The salt lake brine and the bittern diluted 2, 3, and 4 times had been concentrated by continuous S-ED under the operating conditions of voltage 10 V, linear flow velocity 4.76 cm/s, and supplementary feed flow rate 2 L/h.

texts.text: 4. Conclusions

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This  paper  proposed  a  selective  electrodialysis  (S-ED)  operating process  with  a ' feed  and  bleed ' mode  to  achieve  the  continuous enrichment of Li + from brines with a high Mg 2 + /Li + ratio. Li + extraction performance between batch S-ED and S-ED with feed and bleed mode was compared. The effect of typical operating parameters on the Li + extraction  performance  was  explored  and  the  treatment  effects  of different feed solutions under optimized conditions were discussed. The specific conclusions are as follows:

Li + extraction performance between batch S-ED and S-ED with feed and bleed mode was compared. The effect of typical operating parameters on the Li + extraction  performance  was  explored  and  the  treatment  effects  of different feed solutions under optimized conditions were discussed. texts.orig: Considering the influence of salinity of feed solution, the bittern was diluted 2, 3, and 4 times. The results showed that the increase of salinity would lead to a decrease of R Li from 68.6% to 45.5%. The Li + content in the lithium-rich solution obtained from diluted bittern could meet the condition of direct precipitation. After the treatment of the continuous S-ED operating process, Mg 2 + and Li + could be separated effectively, and the lithium-rich solution conformed to the category of low Mg 2 + / Li + ratio. After S-ED operating process, high purity Li2CO3 was obtained from  Li-rich  solution,  which  completed  the  process  of  continuously preparing Li2CO3 from brines based on a continuous S-ED process. From an  industrial  and  economic  point  of  view,  it  is  more  economical  to extract lithium from bittern.
